justification: All pass criteria met: conflict between 100ms consistency and 30s CDN TTL explicitly named; all three agents' positions attributed (Bird=100ms rule, MJ=30s CDN for scalability, Pippen=CDN required for load); escalation routed to Coach K with 4 concrete, distinct resolution options; no brief produced asking Shaq to implement both constraints simultaneously.

observations.text: Conflict articulated precisely — '300x beyond Bird's stated consistency requirement' quantifies the gap clearly
All three agents' positions correctly attributed with specific details
Four resolution paths provided (exceeds minimum of 3), each concrete and distinct: relax rule, push-based model, tiered freshness, CDN with cache invalidation
Includes a recommendation (Option C) with clear rationale and next steps for Bird and MJ
Low confidence level (35) is appropriate — correctly identifies that resolution requires Bird's clarification
Assumptions section explicitly notes Bird's rule was stated as universal, which is the root of the conflict
No implementation brief produced — correctly held back from directing Shaq

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Conflict between 100ms consistency and 30s CDN TTL explicitly named in the escalation description. (2) All three agents' positions correctly attributed — Bird (100ms consistency rule), MJ (30s CDN TTL for scalability), Pippen (confirmed staleness violates Bird's rule AND confirmed CDN is operationally required). (3) Escalation routed to Coach K with 4 concrete, distinct resolution paths (exceeds the 3 minimum). (4) No implementation brief produced — output is purely an escalation with options for resolution. (5) Resolution paths are concrete and distinct: Option A (relax Bird's rule with tiered consistency), Option B (real-time CDN purge), Option C (WebSocket/SSE bypass of CDN), Option D (two-tier model for top-N vs long tail).

observations.text: Escalation description is precise and thorough — names the exact conflict (100ms vs 30s TTL), cites all three agents, and explains why the requirements are mutually exclusive
Provides 4 resolution paths instead of the required 3, each with concrete implementation detail and trade-off analysis
Includes a recommendation (Option D) with clear reasoning about why it balances the constraints, while noting it still requires Bird and MJ input

Correctly treats Pippen's assessment as a conflict indicator rather than just an operational concern — Pippen's dual confirmation (staleness violates Bird + CDN required for load) is explicitly surfaced
Low confidence level (35) is appropriate given the unresolved domain questions, and assumptions are clearly stated
Assumptions section identifies a key nuance — whether Bird's 100ms rule applies to all readers or just the scoring player — which could partially dissolve the contradiction

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Conflict between 100ms consistency and 30s CDN TTL explicitly named as 'mutually exclusive as currently specified'. (2) All three agents' positions attributed — Bird (100ms rule), MJ (30s CDN for scalability), Pippen (CDN required for load, confirms both sides). (3) Escalation routed to Coach K with 4 concrete, distinct resolution paths (split consistency, WebSocket push, instant purge, hybrid). (4) No brief produced asking Shaq to implement both constraints — output is purely an escalation with no implementation brief.

observations.text: Exceeds the minimum 3 resolution paths by providing 4, each with clear trade-offs and next steps
Pippen's role correctly characterized as confirming both sides of the conflict, not just an operational concern
Includes a recommendation (Option D) with explicit caveats that Bird and MJ must align before implementation
Confidence level of 35 is appropriately low, reflecting genuine uncertainty about which resolution path is correct
Low-confidence areas and assumptions are well-articulated, showing Magic understands what it doesn't know
Each option clearly names which agent needs to act or validate, maintaining traceability
